DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE
ORDER OVERRULING DEMURRERS

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING ON THE DEMURRER, WHILE STRIKING THE ADMONITION AGAINST DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL. DEFENDANT EQUITY ONE REALTY & MANAGEMENT CA, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE COMPLAINT IS NOT UNCERTAIN. (CCP 430.10(F).) PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE STANDING AS PLAINTIFF IS ALLEGED TO BE ONE OF THE PARTIES TO THE EXTENSION AGREEMENT. (CCP 367.) FOR THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE BREACH OF THE EXTENSION AGREEMENT. THE EXTENSION AGREEMENT WAS CONFIRMED IN WRITING AND EXTENDED THE TERMS OF THE JOINT VENTURE AGREEMENT BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT. (COMPLAINT, P. 3:11-14, 3:20-26.) THE JOINT VENTURE AGREEMENT IS ATTACHED AS COMPLAINT, EXHIBIT A. TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFENDANT DEMURS TO THE ALLEGATIONS OF PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL, ACCORD AND SATISFACTION, SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE, INJUNCTION, AND RESTITUTION IN THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, A DEMURRER CANNOT BE USED TO ATTACK ONLY PART OF A CAUSE OF ACTION. FOR THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE. WHETHER DAMAGES ARE AN ADEQUATE REMEDY INVOLVES A QUESTION OF FACT THAT IS NOT THE PROPER SUBJECT OF A DEMURRER. FOR THE SECOND AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION, DEFENDANT PREDICATES ITS ARGUMENT ON THE SUSTAINING OF THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION, WHICH HAVE BEEN OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.
ANSWER TO CROSS COMPLAINT FILED BY CROSS DEFENDANT RIDER LIMITED PARTNERSHIP

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-04-2014 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. JUDGE CYNTHIA M. LEE; Clerk JLee. Proceedings not reported. (610)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-17-2014 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Oct-28-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-06-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Giovanna Ferrari, Esq. of Seyfarth Shaw LLP, Tel. 415.397.2823, for defendant and moving party;
Marshall C. Wallace, Esq. of Allen Matkins, Tel. 415.273.7457, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on the demurrer, while striking the admonition against defendant's counsel. DEFENDANT EQUITY ONE REALTY & MANAGEMENT CA, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Overruled. The Complaint is not uncertain. (CCP 430.10(f).) Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege standing as Plaintiff is alleged to be one of the parties to the Extension Agreement. (CCP 367.) For the first cause of action, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege breach of the Extension Agreement. The Extension Agreement was confirmed in writing and extended the terms of the Joint Venture Agreement between Plaintiff and Defendant. (Complaint, p. 3:11-14, 3:20-26.) The Joint Venture Agreement is attached as Complaint, Exhibit A. To the extent that Defendant demurs to the allegations of promissory estoppel, accord and satisfaction, specific performance, injunction, and restitution in the first cause of action, a demurrer cannot be used to attack only part of a cause of action. For the third cause of action, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege specific performance. Whether damages are an adequate remedy involves a question of fact that is not the proper subject of a demurrer. For the second and fourth causes of action, Defendant predicates its argument on the sustaining of the demurrer to the first and third causes of action, which have been overruled. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

Motion For Preference In Setting Trial
